name: Berkshire Hathaway Inc. Class B
summary: Berkshire Hathaway Inc. Class B (BRKB, Financials) — composite stance NEUTRAL / WATCH. Latest reported quarter FY2026 Q1: revenue $93.67B (+4.4% YoY). Valuation 22.0x forward P/E (+36% vs peers). Spot $484.06 (below 200-DMA).
bull: Revenue mix re-accelerating — back half of the trailing 4Q stack averaging +3.5% above the front half. Both NII and fee income lines are contributing, reducing reliance on a single revenue lever.

Range-positioning balanced — at 45% of the 52-week range ($459.11–$514.31), the setup is neither at a momentum-exhaustion ceiling nor at a capitulation floor. Risk/reward into the next catalyst window screens reasonable.
bear: Trend rolling over — spot $484.06 sits -1.2% relative to the 200-DMA ($489.82). The break-down of long-term trend often precedes earnings disappointment by 1–2 quarters as smart-money distribution leads the fundamental print.

Valuation premium — 22.0x forward P/E vs peer median of 16.2x — a 36% premium. The setup requires continued out-execution to maintain; any reversion to peer-median multiples compresses the stock meaningfully even before any earnings revision.
trigger_up: Loan-loss provisions decline or normalize on next earnings — credit-cycle tailwind to bottom line.
Management raises FY guide on next quarterly call — direct upward revision to consensus.
trigger_down: CET1 ratio prints below 11.0% on next quarter — capital constraint reduces buyback authority.
Management cuts FY guide on next quarterly call — direct downward revision; historically sees 5–15% drawdown on print.
Decisive break below the 52-week low at $459.11 — technical confirmation that the long-term trend has rolled over.
bottom_line:
NEUTRAL / WATCH. Berkshire Hathaway Inc. Class B (BRKB) printed $93.67B in total revenue in FY2026 Q1 (+4.4% YoY). At 22.0x forward P/E, the stock carries a 36% premium to peers (16.2x median) — the multiple needs continued execution to defend.

Technical regime is impaired: spot $484.06 below the 200-DMA ($489.82, -1.2%) — long-term trend has rolled over. Sell-side consensus PT of $510 implies +5.4% upside. 30-day implied move is ±4.3%; 90-day is ±8.9%. Stay on the watchlist; wait for either a fundamental inflection (acceleration or margin expansion) or a valuation reset before initiating.
